# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Istio mesh configuration (meshConfig) that allows outbound traffic to any external service. Set outboundTrafficPolicy mode to ALLOW_ANY so services can call external APIs without ServiceEntry definitions. This provides maximum flexibility for microservices that integrate with various external APIs.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent service mesh security.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: "networking.istio.io/v1alpha3"
kind: "MeshPolicy"
metadata: 
 name: "default"   # Name of the policy, it's required for Istio to know which one is active in a given namespace and where are its resources stored (in this case we use default)
namespace: <your-ns>    # Namespace you want your meshPolicy resource be created. You can replace with 'default', or any other name if it's not the current context of Kubernetes cluster 
spec:  
 selector: {}     // This is where all outbound traffic will go, no specific service selected here means ALL services in namespace are allowed to communicate via this policy (allows external APIs)    # You can replace with '*', or select a particular Service if you want only that one. 
 action:  
 - OUTPUT:<your-ns>/default [TARGET_PORT]     // This is where we specify the outbound traffic, it's an example of how to allow all external services in namespace 'myNamespace', and use default service port (5001 by default)  # You can replace with any other valid Istio Service or Port.
